input.code:
# Analyze statistics by sensor
print("Temperature statistics by sensor:")
print(df.groupby('sensor_id')['temperature'].describe())
print("\n")

print("Humidity statistics by sensor:")
print(df.groupby('sensor_id')['humidity'].describe())
print("\n")

print("Status by sensor:")
print(pd.crosstab(df['sensor_id'], df['status']))

# Sensor 3 has significantly different temperature statistics
# Let's investigate further

# Calculate coefficient of variation (CV) for each sensor
cv_temp = df.groupby('sensor_id')['temperature'].agg(['mean', 'std'])
cv_temp['cv'] = (cv_temp['std'] / cv_temp['mean']) * 100
print("Temperature Coefficient of Variation (%):")
print(cv_temp)
print("\n")

# Check for outliers using IQR method
def detect_outliers_iqr(data):
    Q1 = data.quantile(0.25)
    Q3 = data.quantile(0.75)
    IQR = Q3 - Q1
    lower_bound = Q1 - 1.5 * IQR
    upper_bound = Q3 + 1.5 * IQR
    outliers = ((data < lower_bound) | (data > upper_bound)).sum()
    return outliers

print("Temperature outliers by sensor (IQR method):")
for sensor in df['sensor_id'].unique():
    sensor_data = df[df['sensor_id'] == sensor]['temperature']
    outliers = detect_outliers_iqr(sensor_data)
    print(f"{sensor}: {outliers} outliers")

input.code:
# Sensor 3 has much higher variability (CV = 13.8% vs ~1.3% for others)
# Let's look at the time series to see the pattern

df['timestamp'] = pd.to_datetime(df['timestamp'])

# Create a comprehensive visualization
fig, axes = plt.subplots(3, 1, figsize=(14, 10))

# Plot 1: Temperature over time for all sensors
for sensor in df['sensor_id'].unique():
    sensor_data = df[df['sensor_id'] == sensor].sort_values('timestamp')
    axes[0].plot(sensor_data['timestamp'], sensor_data['temperature'], 
                 label=sensor, alpha=0.7, linewidth=1.5 if sensor == 'sensor_3' else 1)

axes[0].set_xlabel('Date', fontsize=11)
axes[0].set_ylabel('Temperature (°C)', fontsize=11)
axes[0].set_title('Temperature Readings Over Time - All Sensors', fontsize=13, fontweight='bold')
axes[0].legend(loc='upper right')
axes[0].grid(True, alpha=0.3)

# Plot 2: Humidity over time for all sensors
for sensor in df['sensor_id'].unique():
    sensor_data = df[df['sensor_id'] == sensor].sort_values('timestamp')
    axes[1].plot(sensor_data['timestamp'], sensor_data['humidity'], 
                 label=sensor, alpha=0.7, linewidth=1.5 if sensor == 'sensor_3' else 1)

axes[1].set_xlabel('Date', fontsize=11)
axes[1].set_ylabel('Humidity (%)', fontsize=11)
axes[1].set_title('Humidity Readings Over Time - All Sensors', fontsize=13, fontweight='bold')
axes[1].legend(loc='upper right')
axes[1].grid(True, alpha=0.3)

# Plot 3: Box plots for temperature comparison
sensor_temps = [df[df['sensor_id'] == sensor]['temperature'].values 
                for sensor in sorted(df['sensor_id'].unique())]
bp = axes[2].boxplot(sensor_temps, labels=sorted(df['sensor_id'].unique()), 
                     patch_artist=True, showmeans=True)

# Color sensor_3 differently
for i, patch in enumerate(bp['boxes']):
    if i == 2:  # sensor_3
        patch.set_facecolor('red')
        patch.set_alpha(0.5)
    else:
        patch.set_facecolor('lightblue')
        patch.set_alpha(0.5)

axes[2].set_xlabel('Sensor ID', fontsize=11)
axes[2].set_ylabel('Temperature (°C)', fontsize=11)
axes[2].set_title('Temperature Distribution by Sensor', fontsize=13, fontweight='bold')
axes[2].grid(True, alpha=0.3, axis='y')

plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('sensor_analysis.png', dpi=300, bbox_inches='tight')
print("Visualization saved as 'sensor_analysis.png'")
plt.close()
